steps.title: Convert percentage changes to multipliers
steps.content: A 184% increase means multiply by $1 + 1.84 = 2.84$. A 28% decrease means multiply by $1 - 0.28 = 0.72$.

steps.title: Chain the multipliers across both years
steps.content: Starting with value $V$ at end 2019, the collectible's value is multiplied by 2.84 in 2020, then by 0.72 in 2021: $$V \times 2.84 \times 0.72 = V \times (2.84 \times 0.72) = V \times 2.0448$$

steps.title: Calculate the net percentage increase
steps.content: The value changed from $V$ to $2.0448V$, a factor of 2.0448. The net percentage increase is $(2.0448 - 1) \times 100\% = 1.0448 \times 100\% = 104.48\%$. A is correct.
